tasks.title: Analyze current product capabilities and architecture
tasks.description: Conduct a comprehensive review of the existing product codebase, documentation, and features. Document: 1) Core functionalities and capabilities, 2) Target use cases and user workflows, 3) Technical architecture and key components, 4) Current limitations or pain points observed in the code/docs, 5) Unique value propositions. Output should be a detailed analysis that serves as the baseline for comparison.

tasks.title: Research and document Claude Code features and capabilities
tasks.description: Research Claude Code (Anthropic's coding assistant) thoroughly. Document: 1) Key features and capabilities, 2) User interface and interaction patterns, 3) Integration points and workflows, 4) Unique differentiators, 5) Known strengths and weaknesses based on public information, reviews, and documentation. Focus on features that would be relevant for competitive analysis.

tasks.title: Research and document GitHub Copilot CLI features and capabilities
tasks.description: Research GitHub Copilot CLI thoroughly. Document: 1) Key features and capabilities, 2) Command-line interface patterns and user experience, 3) Integration with development workflows, 4) Shell and terminal features, 5) Unique differentiators and strengths. Focus on CLI-specific features and developer experience aspects that would inform competitive positioning.

tasks.title: Research and document Roo Code features and capabilities
tasks.description: Research Roo Code (or Roo Cline) thoroughly. Document: 1) Key features and capabilities, 2) User interface and interaction models, 3) Autonomous coding capabilities and agent behaviors, 4) Integration patterns and tool usage, 5) Unique differentiators and innovative features. Focus on aspects that represent emerging trends in AI coding assistants.

tasks.title: Perform gap analysis and identify missing features
tasks.description: Using the outputs from s1, s2, s3, and s4, conduct a comprehensive gap analysis. Identify: 1) Features present in competitors but missing from our product, 2) Features where competitors have superior implementations, 3) Emerging capabilities that represent market trends, 4) User experience improvements observed in competitor products, 5) Integration opportunities we're missing. Prioritize gaps by impact (high/medium/low) and implementation complexity (high/medium/low). Create a structured list of recommended feature additions with rationale for each.

tasks.title: Generate implementation plan with milestones and tasks
tasks.description: Based on the gap analysis from s5 and the current product understanding from s1, create a detailed implementation plan. Structure it with: 1) Strategic phases/milestones (e.g., Phase 1: Core Feature Parity, Phase 2: Differentiation, Phase 3: Innovation), 2) For each milestone: specific features to implement, estimated effort/complexity, dependencies between features, success criteria, 3) Detailed task breakdowns for each feature (technical tasks, testing requirements, documentation needs), 4) Recommended sequencing based on user value and technical dependencies. Format as a clear roadmap with timelines if possible.

tasks.title: Compile SWARMOUT.md document with all findings and recommendations
tasks.description: Create the final SWARMOUT.md document by synthesizing outputs from all previous tasks (s1-s6). Structure the document with: 1) Executive Summary of findings, 2) Current Product Assessment (from s1), 3) Competitive Landscape Analysis (from s2, s3, s4), 4) Gap Analysis and Feature Recommendations (from s5) - organized by priority, 5) Implementation Plan with Milestones and Tasks (from s6) - presented as an actionable roadmap, 6) Conclusion with strategic recommendations. Ensure the document is well-formatted in markdown, easy to navigate, and provides clear actionable insights for stakeholders.
